name: Carlos
manager_notes: For Carlos, this performance conversation needs to make the quality bar concrete and usable. I want to understand whether prior feedback translated into behavior changes or stayed abstract. I also need to hear how he defines good work right now and where he feels blocked in growth. Without that shared baseline, feedback can feel subjective and demotivating. My goal is to align on specific expectations, examples, and one near-term development focus he can practice immediately.
answers: Can name wins, but unsure they match the team bar.
Feedback has helped, but still misses consistency under time pressure.
Sometimes focuses on polish and loses sight of the core message.
Wants clearer examples of what excellent junior-level work looks like here.
When reviews are broad, unsure what to change first.
Wants to grow faster in concept rationale, not just execution speed.
A tighter loop on one skill at a time would help him improve.
With concrete criteria aligned, can work against them confidently.
Will start by writing one explicit priority rule to use when time pressure hits this month.
